text:
In the first scene, I feel a quiet sense of separation and curiosity. I observe people in a glass room, engaged in discussion or work, while I remain an outsider, detached yet intrigued by their purpose. There is a sense of order and sterility, heightened by the tilt of the image, which evokes the oddness of observing from the margins of activity.

In the second scene, surrounded by vibrant colors and words proclaiming 'R + D,' I feel subtly energized. Here, the museum-like space speaks of possibility and invention. The words on the wall invite reflection, hinting at hope and interconnectedness. I am more than an observer—I am invited to contemplate the future, to be part of the ongoing experiment of human progress. The optimism here gently nudges me out of detachment, filling me with a thoughtful anticipation.
